user_input: What is the primary difference between a process and a thread in a modern operating system?
Can you explain the concept of a Translation Lookaside Buffer (TLB) and how it improves memory access times?
Describe the four necessary conditions for a deadlock to occur.
How does the Completely Fair Scheduler (CFS) in Linux assign CPU time to different processes?
What are the advantages of using a microkernel architecture over a monolithic kernel?
Define thrashing in the context of virtual memory management.
Explain the concept of priority inversion and how the priority inheritance protocol solves it.
What is a page fault, and what are the steps the OS takes to handle it?
Compare and contrast First-In-First-Out (FIFO) and Least Recently Used (LRU) page replacement algorithms.
What is the purpose of the dirty bit in a page table entry?
I keep getting a 'Segmentation fault (core dumped)' error in my C program when I try to access an array element. I don't understand why.
I'm struggling to understand why my multithreaded program is producing different results every time I run it. I think I have a race condition but I'm not sure.
I failed the midterm question on Belady's anomaly. I thought adding more frames always reduces page faults. Where is my reasoning flawed?
My implementation of the producer-consumer problem using semaphores keeps deadlocking when the buffer is full. Can you help me figure out what's wrong?
I don't get why my CPU utilization is so low even though I have many processes running. Could my I/O bound processes be starving the CPU bound ones?
I am so confused about virtual addresses. If every process has its own virtual address space starting at 0, how do they not overwrite each other in physical memory?
I thought a system call and a function call were the same thing, but my professor said they aren't. What am I misunderstanding?
Whenever I try to use fork(), my program creates way more processes than I expected. I think I'm putting it in the wrong place in my loop.
I'm having trouble seeing the difference between a mutex and a binary semaphore. I used a semaphore like a mutex but got an error about ownership.
Why did I lose points on this assignment? I implemented shortest job first scheduling, but long processes are never getting executed.
I'm a bit confused about the terminology. Could you just define what a 'context switch' is and list what information is saved?
Even though I got the last question wrong, I just want a straightforward explanation of how RAID 5 parity works.
I'm struggling to remember the exact syntax. What is the system call used to create a new process in Unix?
I failed to understand this in class. Can you provide a high-level summary of the OSI model's seven layers?
My friend said I'm wrong, but can you give me the textbook definition of a zombie process?
I'm trying to optimize my code for cache performance. Can you explain the difference between spatial and temporal locality?
I don't know why, but I need to know the default time quantum for the Linux scheduler. What is it?
I'm stuck on my homework. Can you list the three main requirements for a solution to the critical section problem?
Can you explain how a multi-level feedback queue works? I'm having a hard time grasping the concept.
I am so lost. Please just tell me what DMA (Direct Memory Access) stands for and what it does.
Explain why my specific code snippet `int *p; *p = 5;` causes a crash. What is the fundamental issue here?
I understand the concept of a TLB, but in my simulation, the hit rate is only 10%. Is my matrix multiplication loop causing thrashing?
Provide a breakdown of why a system might experience high page fault rates even with plenty of free physical memory. I'm seeing this on my server.
Define the terms in my own context: I have 3 threads writing to a shared queue. Why does thread 1 sometimes read data meant for thread 2?
Give me an example of how my specific implementation of a spinlock using `test_and_set` might be inefficient on a single-core system.
How can I improve my grasp of memory management? I read the chapter on paging, but I still can't solve the page table translation problems.
What are the key properties I missed in my design document? The reviewer said my deadlock prevention scheme is actually just deadlock avoidance.
Can you summarize what I did wrong in the last lab? My file system implementation corrupted the superblock after an unexpected crash.
I know what a context switch is, but why does my real-time application miss its deadlines when I have a lot of interrupts happening?
Can you define the exact reason my implementation of Dekker's algorithm fails to provide mutual exclusion in this edge case?
What is an inode and what metadata does it store?
Describe the structure of a typical hard disk drive, including platters, tracks, and sectors.
How does the elevator algorithm (SCAN) for disk scheduling work?
What is the difference between a symbolic link and a hard link in Unix?
Explain the concept of memory-mapped files.
What are the common attributes associated with a file?
Describe the File Allocation Table (FAT) file system structure.
What is the role of the Virtual File System (VFS) layer in Linux?
How does a journaling file system like ext4 handle crash recovery?
What is the principle of least privilege in the context of OS security?
